qa_pairs.question: What is the primary function of the `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS` environment variable?
qa_pairs.answer: The `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS` environment variable allows you to define locations for local module trees, which will automatically integrate into the central module tree. It is specifically designed for hierarchical module trees installed using EasyBuild.

qa_pairs.question: How do you set up the `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS` variable and install an EasyBuild recipe to utilize it?
qa_pairs.answer: To use `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS`, first define it by exporting the variable, for example: `export RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS=/opt/software/easybuild/modules`. Afterwards, you can install your EasyBuild recipe using the command `eb --installpath /opt/software/easybuild <your recipe>.eb`.

qa_pairs.question: What type of module trees is the `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS` environment variable intended for?
qa_pairs.answer: The `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS` environment variable is intended exclusively for hierarchical module trees that are installed via EasyBuild, ensuring they follow the same hierarchical structure as the central modules.

qa_pairs.question: When should I use `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS` instead of `module use --priority`?
qa_pairs.answer: `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS` is intended for hierarchical module trees installed through EasyBuild. If you want to add a local path to a flat module tree, you should use `module use --priority 1 /path/to/your/flat/module/tree` (for higher priority) or `module use --priority -1 /path/to/your/flat/module/tree` (for lower priority), ensuring these commands are added after sourcing the main scripts.
